title: How do you use Managed Spot Training in SageMaker without losing hours of progress to interruptions?
prompt:
How do you use Managed Spot Training in SageMaker without losing hours of progress to interruptions?

*Answer in the editor as you would out loud in an interview (a few sentences to a short paragraph is fine). Then use 💡 Hint, 🧠 AI feedback, or 📗 Show answer.*
reference_solution:
The core requirement is checkpointing to durable storage, not local disk. You configure the training job with a checkpoint S3 URI, and your training script needs to actually write checkpoints there periodically and reload from the latest one on startup — without that, spot training just means sometimes losing hours of a job, which defeats the point. Checkpoint frequently enough that an interruption only costs minutes, but not so frequently that checkpoint I/O becomes a bottleneck. Set `max_wait` meaningfully higher than `max_run` so SageMaker has room to retry.

**Takeaway:** Test the interruption-and-resume path deliberately, since a checkpoint that "should" resume correctly but silently resets optimizer state will quietly produce a worse model — your training script owns what "progress" means.
hint_notes: Checkpoint to S3 (not local disk) periodically, reload latest checkpoint on startup, set max_wait > max_run. Deliberately test the interruption/resume path — a checkpoint that silently resets optimizer state quietly produces a worse model.
